market_summary: Third straight losing session for US equities amid a bond rout. S&P 500 -0.67% (7,353), Nasdaq -0.84% (25,870), Dow -0.65% (49,375). 30-year Treasury yield hit 5.198% — highest since July 2007 — on persistent inflation fears tied to Iran-driven oil shock. 10Y yield rose 6bps to 4.687%. Gold fell $78 to $4,489 (-1.7%), silver cratered $4.37 to $73.36 (-5.6%) as rising real yields undercut safe-haven bid. Oil eased (Brent ~$110, WTI ~$103) after Trump cancelled planned Iran strike following Gulf ally intervention. BTC held $76.8K, ETH at $2,296. India hiked gold/silver import duty from 6% to 15%, adding structural headwind to physical demand.
key_events_tomorrow: 106 economic events scheduled for May 20 — busy data day, check econoday.com for specifics
40 earnings reports on May 20 — no mega-cap confirmed but heavy mid-cap slate
US-Iran negotiations: Trump paused attack but Iran rejects latest draft terms — deadlock risk
Treasury yield pressure: 30Y at 5.2%, BofA survey shows 62% of fund managers expect 6% — any further spike hammers risk assets
Japan GDP surprise: Q1 annualized 2.1% vs 1.7% expected — could boost Nikkei, JPY implications for carry trades
UK Hormuz deployment: drones, fighters, warship — any confrontation escalates oil immediately
overnight_risks: Iran deadlock: despite Trump pausing attack, Tehran says US conditions 'overly demanding' — breakdown sends oil back toward $120+
Bond vigilantes: 30Y at 19-year high, 10Y at 4.69% — if Asia/Europe sell continues overnight, equity futures gap down
Silver flash crash risk: -5.6% today, India duty hike removes structural demand, gold-silver ratio widened to 61.2:1
Crypto liquidation continuation: $657M liquidated yesterday, BTC holding $76.8K but 10Y yield pressure could break $75K support
Oil supply: OPEC production still down 9.7M bpd from pre-Iran-war, inventories falling 8.5M b/d in Q2 per EIA
Asia open: Nikkei at 62K+ may react to US bond rout; strong Japan GDP data is double-edged (growth but BoJ tightening fears)

instrument_news.BTC-USD: Holding $76.8K, range-bound $76.5-77K all day. ETF recovery real but incomplete — $3.29B inflows over past 2 months, cumulative $58.72B since launch. SEC approved options on spot BTC ETFs (IBIT covered calls/protective puts now live). Bitcoin Depot filed Chapter 11. Macro headwind from 30Y yield at 5.2% and strong USD. Support $75K, resistance $80K.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: Trading $2,296, below 200-day MA of $2,367, 53% below ATH. BlackRock staked ETH ETF pulled $100M on first day. Exchange supply at yearly low 14.9M ETH. Staking ratio up to 31%. Ronin migrated to Ethereum L2. SBI/Rakuten planning regulated crypto trusts in Japan. Grayscale staking ETF live on NYSE Arca.
instrument_news.MSTR: At ~$165, down from $177 area. Holds 843,738 BTC at avg $66,384 ($33.1B total cost). Recent 24,869 BTC purchase caused 6% stock drop — market punishing aggressive treasury expansion at these BTC prices. Tracking BTC weakness. Stock +35% since Feb but vulnerable if BTC breaks $75K.
instrument_news.XAG-USD: Cratered 5.6% to $73.36 — worst day in weeks. India duty hike from 6% to 15% structurally negative for second-largest consumer. Gold-silver ratio widened to 61.2:1 reflecting industrial demand sensitivity. Rising real yields (30Y at 5.2%) undercut metals broadly. Silver ETF AUM in India rose 7% to ~₹95K crore despite duty — price appreciation not inflows.
instrument_news.XAU-USD: Down $78 to $4,489 (-1.7%), pulled back from $4,512 earlier. India duty hike headline negative. Central bank buying continues but insufficient to offset real yield pressure from 30Y at 5.2%. BofA survey: 62% expect 30Y to hit 6%. At 5%+ real yields, gold faces significant headwinds. Support $4,450, resistance $4,550.

metals_outlook: Metals under serious pressure from dual headwinds: (1) 30Y yield at 19-year high of 5.2% with expectations of 6% — rising real yields are gold's kryptonite; (2) India raised import duty from 6% to 15%, removing structural physical demand from world's #2 consumer. Silver hit harder (-5.6% vs gold -1.7%) as industrial demand expectations fall in restrictive rate environment. Gold-silver ratio widened to 61.2:1. Central bank buying remains a floor but insufficient to offset macro headwinds. Near-term bearish tilt for both metals until yields stabilize or Iran escalation reignites safe-haven bid. Key level: gold $4,450 support, silver $70 psychological.
oil_outlook: Brent eased to ~$110, WTI ~$103 after Trump cancelled planned Iran strike on Gulf allies' request. Strait of Hormuz still effectively closed (5% of pre-conflict shipping). OPEC production down 9.7M bpd from pre-war levels. EIA expects inventories to fall 8.5M b/d in Q2. Iran says US conditions 'overly demanding' — deadlock persists. UK deploying military assets to Hormuz. Any negotiation breakdown immediately retests $120+ Brent. Oil above $100 is structurally inflationary and negative for risk assets. OPEC cut demand growth forecast to 1.2M bpd from 1.4M.
crypto_outlook: BTC range-bound at $76.8K, ETH at $2,296. Bond market is the biggest headwind — 30Y at 5.2% makes risk-free return attractive vs crypto. ETF flows recovering ($3.29B over 2 months) but not enough to overcome macro drag. SEC options approval on spot BTC ETFs adds institutional tools. CLARITY Act progressing toward federal crypto framework by mid-2026. Key risk: if 10Y breaks 5%, crypto likely reprices lower. Support BTC $75K, ETH $2,100. Upside catalyst needed — either yields reversing or Iran deal boosting risk appetite.
sentiment_shift: Decidedly risk-off, driven by the bond market. The 30Y yield hitting a 19-year high is the dominant story — it pressures every risk asset simultaneously. Equities down for third straight session, metals selling off despite geopolitical uncertainty (unusual — means real yield pressure > safe-haven demand), crypto holding but fragile. India gold duty hike adds a structural negative for metals. The only bullish catalyst would be a credible Iran deal (oil drops, yields drop, risk-on) or a sudden Fed pivot signal. Neither appears imminent. Position for continued volatility with downside bias across all instruments.
